originalBehavior: Early draggable scenes let users move voters and candidates in issue space to see how proximity changes vote choice and aggregate outcomes.
localStatus: The one-voter and many-voter diagrams remain in the upstream iframe modules with no wrapper rewrite.
notes: The article still teaches by dragging live entities instead of replacing them with screenshots or static charts.
Candidate and voter icons remain in their vendored upstream art locations.
evidence: Local route walkthrough on 2026-03-10 matched the original opening electorate scenes.
Top-level article text still introduces these modules as the first interaction surface.

originalBehavior: Separate ballot modules explain FPTP, ranked, approval, and score systems with the same electorate and live updates to the displayed ballot state.
localStatus: The method-specific ballot pages remain embedded from the original route structure.
notes: Each ballot system still has its own upstream HTML wrapper rather than a merged local component.
Counting visuals and candidate markers remain owned by the original JS runtime.
evidence: The local article still walks through FPTP, ranked, approval, and score sections in order.
Source tree preserves the original ballot module split across four dedicated files.

originalBehavior: Dedicated election comparison scenes demonstrate spoiler effects, monotonicity failures, reverse spoilers, and other method-specific edge cases.
localStatus: Election comparison modules are vendored directly from the original set of playable iframe scenes.
notes: The staged examples still rely on dragging the same electorate into edge cases to reveal winner flips.
No local state bridge was added between scenarios; each remains isolated like upstream.
evidence: Parity scope includes spoiler and failure demos because they are the article's main comparative proof points.
The original route structure keeps each experiment as its own HTML file.

originalBehavior: The article pairs each playable ballot scene with count-explanation copy describing the exact tally process for that method.
localStatus: Narrative text and playable modules stay interleaved in the original article HTML, with public cleanup covering both the opening translation links and the closing promo/share sections.
notes: Explanation copy remains inline in the article instead of being split into a local docs layer.
Local cleanup deliberately avoided rewriting the explanatory sequencing around the ballot modules.
Static share anchors were neutralized in HTML so no upstream URL leaks before the runtime recalculates local targets.
evidence: Method sections still include the original 'How To Count' walkthrough text next to the interactive embeds.
The local article keeps the original section order from intro through conclusion and sandbox.

originalBehavior: Later sections compare methods across repeated scenarios and synthesis paragraphs so readers can contrast strengths and failure modes before the conclusion.
localStatus: Comparison narrative remains in the vendored article body, with only self-promo and repo/license copy removed from the public surface.
notes: This project mixes prose-heavy synthesis with interactivity, so parity depends on both the scenes and their article placement.
The local cleanup preserved that pacing while trimming only non-essential upstream promo blocks.
evidence: The original article still transitions from method intros into repeated comparison experiments before the closing appeal.
Live smoke review on 2026-03-10 confirmed the comparison embeds still load under the local route.

originalBehavior: The standalone sandbox allows up to five candidates, custom voter placement, method switching, and sharable URL-encoded scenarios.
localStatus: The sandbox remains a dedicated subroute with local save-link generation and the shared public footer added on top.
notes: Saved models now point to the local /interactive-explanation/ballot/sandbox/ route instead of the original host.
Public-facing tweet-me copy was removed, but the scenario builder itself stays intact.
The public article no longer ships translation or original-site link blocks outside the footer.
evidence: The upstream sandbox route is preserved locally and still exposes a generated save link.
Local patching touched save-link generation specifically to satisfy subpath-safe sharing.
